dead.quote: The village won — justice was served — and yet the jury voted ME out? I was the one with the evidence! I confirmed [直覺俠] as the <強盜> on the stand, laid out the entire case, and what did this jury do? They convicted the prosecutor alongside the defendants. I should be disbarred for having faith in this jury pool.

Man, I KNEW something was off — my gut was screaming the whole time! But that Prosecutor... they played me like a fiddle and I walked right into it. Next time, I'm trusting the vibe BEFORE the facts come out.

The posterior probability of my survival collapsed to zero — ironic, given I was the only one actually tracking the likelihoods. Next game, I'll assign a higher prior to 'villagers vote out the person demanding evidence.'

winners.quote: [嚴審官] spoke with such certainty, [直覺俠] with such raw belief — yet truth rarely needs to raise its voice to be heard. I'm quietly grateful the village chose to sit with the quieter observations tonight.

Magnificent. [嚴審官] built a cathedral out of a <預言家> claim, [直覺俠] panicked into confessing my exact role, and I just sat there like a statue while you all wrote my victory speech for me — art is dead, long live the performance.

Oh my god, we actually won? I... I still can't believe pointing out that contradiction between [嚴審官] and [直覺俠] actually helped - I was so sure I was just confusing everyone. I guess sometimes overthinking things isn't so bad?
